commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b845b0d467432bf6f913feb277d3612a8202cebd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds ChatGPT/Codex request-contract headers to the OpenAI boundary-aware responses client and adds helper unit tests for Codex versus native OpenAI Responses models.

PR surface: Source +29, Tests +45. Total +74 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from linked live reports and source inspection: the issue has concrete openclaw infer commands plus a captured status=200, empty-content-type SSE response, and current main's guard rejects that shape. I did not rerun the live ChatGPT OAuth request in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the code repair is provider-contract-sensitive and still needs live ChatGPT subscription proof before merge.

target.title: fix(openai): send Codex request contract headers on the boundary-aware transport
target.body:
## Summary

Since the OpenAI provider-unification refactor (v2026.6.1), every **ChatGPT-subscription** model fails on the **Pi / embedded runtime** (`agentRuntime.id: pi`, the default) with:

```
ProviderHttpError code=invalid_provider_content_type
  (surfaced to the agent as a wrapped OpenAI-SDK "Connection error.")
```

This takes down `provider=openai, api=openai-chatgpt-responses` entirely; bots fail over to other providers. Tracked in #90083 (and dup #90086 / #90269).

## Root cause

`openai-chatgpt-responses` on the Pi runtime is routed through the **generic boundary-aware OpenAI-SDK transport** (`createOpenAIResponsesTransportStreamFn`), not the native Codex provider stream (`streamOpenAICodexResponses`). The native path sends the Codex wire-contract via `buildSSEHeaders()` / `buildBaseCodexHeaders()` (`src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.ts`), but the boundary-aware client (`createOpenAIResponsesClient` → `buildOpenAIClientHeaders`) emits only the OpenClaw attribution headers and **omits the two headers the `chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex` endpoint requires**:

- `chatgpt-account-id` (derived from the OAuth JWT via `resolveOpenAICodexAccountId`)
- `OpenAI-Beta: responses=experimental`

Without `chatgpt-account-id`, the endpoint answers an authenticated request with a **non-stream (HTML) `200`**. `assertOpenAISdkStreamContentType` (`src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts`) then throws `invalid_provider_content_type` because the body is neither `text/event-stream` nor JSON; the OpenAI SDK re-wraps the throw from inside its `fetch` as a generic `APIConnectionError` ("Connection error."), which is why logs show a connection error whose `cause` is `invalid_provider_content_type`.

This is the gap the boundary-aware fallback's own comment anticipated: *"Keep OpenAI-family APIs here until PI's native HTTP streams preserve the same OpenClaw request contract."*

## Fix

Inject the missing Codex contract headers in `createOpenAIResponsesClient` (where the resolved `apiKey` is available), gated on `isOpenAICodexResponsesModel(model)`:

- adds `OpenAI-Beta: responses=experimental`
- adds `chatgpt-account-id` when it can be decoded from the OAuth JWT (omitted gracefully otherwise)

The gate keys strictly on `provider=openai` + `api ∈ {openai-chatgpt-responses, openclaw-openai-responses-transport}`, so `api.openai.com` (`openai-responses`), Azure OpenAI, and every other provider/model are unchanged. The existing request body already runs through `sanitizeOpenAICodexResponsesParams`, so headers were the only remaining gap.

## Testing

- Added unit tests for `buildOpenAICodexResponsesContractHeaders`:
  - ChatGPT-subscription model → both headers present (account-id decoded from the JWT)
  - native `api.openai.com` responses model → no Codex headers injected
- Verified the JWT-decode + header logic locally against the real `resolveOpenAICodexAccountId`.

> ⚠️ **Note for maintainers:** I could not reproduce the end-to-end request against the live Cloudflare-fronted `chatgpt.com` endpoint in my environment, so the header set is derived from the native path (`buildSSEHeaders`) rather than confirmed against a live subscription request. A maintainer with ChatGPT-subscription credentials should confirm the live fix. Unauthenticated probes to the endpoint return a clean `401 application/json` (Cloudflare is not blocking on request signature), which is consistent with the failure being the missing `chatgpt-account-id` on the *authenticated* request rather than an edge/bot block.

Fixes #90083
target.author: aaajiao
